query:
Modeling's `separability_matrix` does not compute separability correctly for nested CompoundModels
Consider the following model:

```python
from astropy.modeling import models as m
from astropy.modeling.separable import separability_matrix

cm = m.Linear1D(10) & m.Linear1D(5)
```

It's separability matrix as you might expect is a diagonal:

```python
>>> separability_matrix(cm)
array([[ True, False],
       [False,  True]])
```

If I make the model more complex:
```python
>>> separability_matrix(m.Pix2Sky_TAN() & m.Linear1D(10) & m.Linear1D(5))
array([[ True,  True, False, False],
       [ True,  True, False, False],
       [False, False,  True, False],
       [False, False, False,  True]])
```

The output matrix is again, as expected, the outputs and inputs to the linear models are separable and independent of each other.

If however, I nest these compound models:
```python
>>> separability_matrix(m.Pix2Sky_TAN() & cm)
array([[ True,  True, False, False],
       [ True,  True, False, False],
       [False, False,  True,  True],
       [False, False,  True,  True]])
```
Suddenly the inputs and outputs are no longer separable?

This feels like a bug to me, but I might be missing something?
memory_items:
```
# Memory Item 1
## Title Fix nested CompoundModel separability in _cstack
## Description Correct the _cstack function to properly handle nested CompoundModels by copying separability matrix values instead of setting them to 1
## Content When the right operand of the '&' operator is a matrix (from recursive separability calculation), the code was incorrectly setting all values to 1 instead of copying the actual separability information. The fix changes `cright[-right.shape[0]:, -right.shape[1]:] = 1` to `cright[-right.shape[0]:, -right.shape[1]:] = right` to preserve the separability matrix values.

# Memory Item 2
## Title Understand _cstack matrix handling for recursive calls
## Description The _cstack function handles both Model objects and pre-computed matrices differently - Models get processed through _coord_matrix while matrices need proper positioning and value copying
## Content When _cstack is called during recursive separability calculation, the operands are matrices (results of previous _separable calls) rather than Model objects. For matrices, the function creates a zero matrix of the correct size and copies the input matrix values into the correct position. This ensures that separability information from nested compound models is preserved correctly.

# Memory Item 3
## Title Verify nested compound model separability with test cases
## Description Create comprehensive test cases to verify that nested CompoundModels produce the same separability results as their flat equivalents
## Content When fixing separability issues with nested CompoundModels, test multiple scenarios: simple compound models, flat compound models with non-separable components (like Pix2Sky_TAN), nested compound models, deeply nested structures (3-4 levels), and mixed scenarios. Verify that separability_matrix and is_separable produce identical results for equivalent flat and nested compound model configurations.
```

query:
Please support header rows in RestructuredText output
### Description

It would be great if the following would work:

```Python
>>> from astropy.table import QTable
>>> import astropy.units as u
>>> import sys
>>> tbl = QTable({'wave': [350,950]*u.nm, 'response': [0.7, 1.2]*u.count})
>>> tbl.write(sys.stdout,  format="ascii.rst")
===== ========
 wave response
===== ========
350.0      0.7
950.0      1.2
===== ========
>>> tbl.write(sys.stdout,  format="ascii.fixed_width", header_rows=["name", "unit"])
|  wave | response |
|    nm |       ct |
| 350.0 |      0.7 |
| 950.0 |      1.2 |
>>> tbl.write(sys.stdout,  format="ascii.rst", header_rows=["name", "unit"])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/astropy/table/connect.py", line 129, in __call__
    self.registry.write(instance, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/astropy/io/registry/core.py", line 369, in write
    return writer(data, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/astropy/io/ascii/connect.py", line 26, in io_write
    return write(table, filename, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/astropy/io/ascii/ui.py", line 856, in write
    writer = get_writer(Writer=Writer, fast_writer=fast_writer, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/astropy/io/ascii/ui.py", line 800, in get_writer
    writer = core._get_writer(Writer, fast_writer, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/astropy/io/ascii/core.py", line 1719, in _get_writer
    writer = Writer(**writer_kwargs)
TypeError: RST.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'header_rows'
```


### Additional context

RestructuredText output is a great way to fill autogenerated documentation with content, so having this flexible makes the life easier `:-)`
memory_items: All edge cases are working correctly! Now let me verify that the existing tests still pass to make sure I haven't broken anything:

```bash
cd /testbed && python -m pytest astropy/io/ascii/tests/test_rst.py -xvs
```
<returncode>4</returncode>
<output>
ERROR: file/dir not found: astropy/io/ascii/tests/test_rst.py
</returncode>
